claim.content:
For twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) the noninteracting low-energy Dirac-point band Fermi velocity v_F^*(θ) produced by the Bistritzer–MacDonald continuum moiré-band model is strongly suppressed as the twist angle θ (measured in degrees) approaches the largest magic angle θ_M (the angle at which the noninteracting Dirac-point band velocity vanishes), and for 0<θ<3° this noninteracting moiré-band velocity is well described by the empirical linear approximation
      $$
      v_F^*(\theta)\approx 0.5\,|\theta-\theta_M|\,v_F,
      $$
      where v_F^*(θ) is the tBLG moiré-band Dirac-cone Fermi velocity, θ and θ_M are expressed in degrees, and v_F ≈ 10^8 cm/s is the monolayer-graphene Dirac velocity; this linear fit reproduces the numerical outputs of the continuum Bistritzer–MacDonald moiré-band calculation to useful accuracy in the stated angular window.

conclusion.content:
For twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) the noninteracting low-energy Dirac-point band Fermi velocity v_F^*(θ) produced by the Bistritzer–MacDonald continuum moiré-band model is strongly suppressed as the twist angle θ (measured in degrees) approaches the largest magic angle θ_M (the angle at which the noninteracting Dirac-point band velocity vanishes), and for 0<θ<3° this noninteracting moiré-band velocity is well described by the empirical linear approximation
      $$
      v_F^*(\theta)\approx 0.5\,|\theta-\theta_M|\,v_F,
      $$
      where v_F^*(θ) is the tBLG moiré-band Dirac-cone Fermi velocity, θ and θ_M are expressed in degrees, and v_F ≈ 10^8 cm/s is the monolayer-graphene Dirac velocity; this linear fit reproduces the numerical outputs of the continuum Bistritzer–MacDonald moiré-band calculation to useful accuracy in the stated angular window.

premises.content:
For twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG) with twist angle θ measured in degrees, let θ_M denote the largest magic angle at which the noninteracting continuum moiré-band Dirac-point band velocity vanishes, and let v_F ≈ 10^8 cm/s be the monolayer-graphene Dirac velocity; then the noninteracting continuum Bistritzer–MacDonald moiré-band calculation of the low-energy Dirac-point Fermi velocity v_F^*(θ) is accurately approximated for 0<θ<3° by the linear formula
      $$
      v_F^*(\theta)\approx 0.5\,|\theta-\theta_M|\,v_F,
      $$
      with θ and θ_M in degrees, so that the numerical continuum-band outputs for v_F^*(θ) are captured to useful accuracy by this linear fit in the specified angular window.
      R. Bistritzer and A.H. MacDonald, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 108 (2011) 12233; F. Wu, E. Hwang, S. Das Sarma, Phys. Rev. B 99 (2019) 165112.

The Bistritzer–MacDonald continuum moiré-band model, a continuum effective model parameterized by intralayer graphene parameters and interlayer tunneling amplitudes, provides the noninteracting low-energy band structure of twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG), and the Dirac-point band Fermi velocity v_F^*(θ) obtained from that noninteracting continuum model is to be treated as the bare (noninteracting) input parameter for subsequent low-energy many-body and phonon-transport calculations.
      R. Bistritzer and A.H. MacDonald, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. USA 108 (2011) 12233.

steps.reasoning:
Define $v_F^*(\theta)$ as the noninteracting low-energy Dirac-point Fermi velocity of twisted bilayer graphene (tBLG), where $\theta$ is the twist angle expressed in degrees and $\theta_M$ denotes the largest magic angle at which the noninteracting Dirac velocity vanishes (i.e., $v_F^*(\theta_M)=0$); the monolayer graphene (MLG) bare Dirac velocity is $v_F\approx 10^{8}\ \mathrm{cm/s}$. 
Fig. 1(a)

State the continuum moiré-band calculation basis: the Bistritzer–MacDonald continuum moiré-band model (band-structure model) is used to compute $v_F^*(\theta)$ as a function of $\theta$, and those calculated values are presented as the tBLG Dirac velocity at the Dirac point versus twist angle. The continuum calculation details are those cited from the band-structure model references and prior work.
[54]
[36]

Report the empirical approximation extracted from the continuum moiré-band calculation: for twist angles $\theta<3^\circ$ the computed noninteracting tBLG Dirac velocity is well approximated by the linear formula
$$
v_F^*(\theta)\approx 0.5\,|\theta-\theta_M|\,v_F,
$$
where both $\theta$ and $\theta_M$ are measured in degrees, and $v_F\approx 10^8\ \mathrm{cm/s}$. This empirical expression is the explicit formula appearing in the continuum calculation discussion.
[36]
Fig. 1(a)

Explain the domain of validity of the empirical approximation: the authors state the approximation holds for $\theta<3^\circ$, while for $\theta>3^\circ$ one may take $v_F^*(\theta)\approx v_F$ for the purposes of their low-energy continuum theory; the number $\theta_M$ from their calculation is $\theta_M\approx 1.02^\circ$ as reported in their continuum-band evaluation.
[36]

Explicitly connect $v_F^*(\theta)$ to subsequent low-energy theories: define the Coulomb fine-structure coupling $\alpha$ in terms of $v_F^*$ by $\alpha=e^2/(\kappa\hbar v_F^*)$, where $e$ is the elementary charge, $\hbar$ is the reduced Planck constant, and $\kappa$ is the background dielectric constant; also state that $v_F^*(\theta)$ is the bare (noninteracting) low-energy parameter entering the continuum electron-phonon resistivity formula (the resistivity formula uses $v_F^{*2}$ in the denominator). Thus the empirical approximation for $v_F^*(\theta)$ summarizes the noninteracting Bistritzer–MacDonald moiré-band calculation used throughout the paper to set band-velocity inputs for both phonon and Coulomb interaction theories.

motivating_questions.content: In the low-energy continuum description of tBLG the magnitude of electronic interaction effects (both electron-phonon and electron-electron) is governed principally by the bare band Dirac velocity $v_F^*(\theta)$. Prior to this empirical relation there was not a concise, directly usable parametrization of how the moiré band Dirac velocity depends on twist angle in the small-angle regime for use in many-body estimates. Without such a relation one cannot quantitatively predict the scaling of the phonon-induced resistivity [which contains $v_F^{*2}$ in the denominator] or the effective Coulomb coupling $\alpha\propto 1/v_F^*$ as a function of twist angle, making direct theory–experiment comparison ambiguous. The approximate linear-in-$|\theta-\theta_M|$ formula gives a simple, physically transparent bridge from moiré band calculations to continuum many-body estimates (e.g., for $v_F^*$ entering Eqs. (1),(13),(21)), enabling evaluation of when continuum Dirac treatments should produce large interaction effects and where they break down.

paper:812646407474249731.en_title: Electron–phonon and electron–electron interaction effects in twisted bilayer graphene
